data.title: YouTube Targets 'AI Slop' and Deepfakes as Top Priorities for 2026
data.description:
Neal Mohan, the CEO of video-sharing platform YouTube, said that cutting down on low-quality "AI slop" and improving deepfake detection will be major priorities in 2026. In his annual letter, Mohan warned that it is becoming increasingly difficult to tell the difference between real and AI-generated content, especially when it comes to deepfake videos. Furthermore, as artificial intelligence becomes more widespread, YouTube's parent company, tech giant Google (GOOGL), continues to invest heavily in AI infrastructure and models, while YouTube faces a surge in AI-created video uploads.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unfortunately, this surge includes a flood of low-quality AI videos that are designed to game recommendation algorithms. As a result, Mohan said that YouTube is responding by improving its spam and clickbait-fighting systems, while clearly labeling AI-generated content and requiring creators to disclose when videos have been altered. The platform also removes harmful synthetic media that violates its rules and is building tools that detect when a creator's likeness is used without permission.

At the same time, YouTube wants to continue supporting creators rather than replacing them with AI. Indeed, Mohan said that more than one million channels used YouTube's AI creation tools each day in December, and the company plans to expand these features across formats like Shorts. Creators will soon also be able to generate content using their own likeness, create games from text prompts, and experiment with music. In addition, YouTube is focused on helping creators earn more through shopping, brand deals, and fan-funding tools, while making the platform safer and easier to use.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 27 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $343.65 per share implies 3.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump's Tariffs Have 'Come at the Expense of the American People,' Says Citadel's Ken Griffin
data.description:
Citadel CEO and founder Ken Griffin said on Tuesday that U.S. consumers have been paying the price for the Trump administration's tariffs.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Unfortunately, the most recent research indicates the tax been borne by both American consumers and by American businesses," he said at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland. "So, the money raised by Washington in the tariffs has really come at the expense of the American people."

Americans Pay for 96% of Tariffs, Shows Kiel Study

A study released by the Kiel Institute earlier this week found that Americans have absorbed 96% of the Trump administration's tariffs, while foreign exporters have incurred the remaining 4%. The German research firm analyzed shipment-level data across 25 million transactions worth nearly $4 trillion.

Kiel pointed to two event studies on Brazil and India, two countries that received relatively high tariff rates from the U.S. Export data showed that the tariffs didn't cause export prices to fall, and instead lowered trade volume. In addition, the study said that tariffs interfere with supply chains and buying habits while narrowing the selection of goods available for consumers.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Rallies on Reports of Increased GPU Prices
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock was up on Wednesday alongside reports that it has increased the price of the graphics processing units (GPUs) and VRAM that it sells to graphics card manufacturers. While unconfirmed by Nvidia, insiders claim that the semiconductor company notified GPU makers of a price increase for these bundles on January 16.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest report doesn't include the exact price increase for Nvidia's GPU and VRAM bundles, but it does claim that Nvidia's price is still below what rival Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) charges graphics card makers for its GPU and VRAM bundles. The price increase hasn't affected the MSRP of Nvidia cards yet, but talk from the company's executives earlier this year suggests that consumers might see a price change sometime in 2026.

Consumers have had to deal with increased PC parts prices over the past couple of months due to the ongoing RAMpocalypse. The demand for RAM has surged alongside increased demand for artificial intelligence (AI) data centers. This has resulted in a shortage as more server components are needed to meet this demand, limiting what's available to retail customers.

Nvidia Stock Movement Today

Nvidia stock was up 1.53% on Wednesday but was down 3% year-to-date. Even so, the stock has rallied 21.08% over the past 12 months.

NVDA stock activity today saw some 36.47 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 153.34 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Why AMD Stock Is Up Today — and What a 5-Star Analyst Sees Next
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose Wednesday after the chipmaker announced changes to its Board of Directors. The company appointed KC McClure to its board, adding deep finance experience. Meanwhile, in a note today, top KeyBanc analyst John Vinh reiterated a Buy rating on AMD and kept his $270 price target. He expects stronger fourth-quarter results and higher guidance, driven by solid server demand and growing momentum in AI.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Top Analyst Is Upbeat About AMD Stock

Vinh said demand for AMD's server CPUs remains very strong, helped by its latest-generation Turin chips. According to the analyst, large cloud customers are already locking in capacity for 2026, and AMD is close to being sold out of server CPUs through the end of that year.

Because of this tight supply, Vinh said AMD is considering price increases of around 10% to 15%, which could further support revenue and margins.

On the AI side, Vinh said revenue visibility is improving. He expects AMD's AI GPU revenue to reach $14 billion to $15 billion in 2026, supported by rising shipments of its MI300-series successors.

Looking ahead, Vinh expects better results and stronger guidance. He forecasts fourth-quarter revenue of $9.75 billion and EPS of $1.36. Both are above Wall Street estimates. For the first quarter of 2026, he expects revenue of $9.75 billion and EPS of $1.35.

What Investors Are Watching

Vinh said sentiment on AMD is mixed. Bulls highlight strong server CPU demand and steady progress in AI, while bears worry about GPU volumes and tough competition from Nvidia (NVDA).

Looking ahead, he said investors will be focused on three things:

  1. the production timeline and customer commitments for AMD's MI455 and Helios systems
  2. updates on AI revenue growth in 2025 and 2026, and
  3. supply and demand trends in the traditional server market.

Is AMD Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for AMD is Strong Buy, based on 26 Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AMD stock price target of $284.29, representing a potential 22.58% upside for the shares.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 1/21/2026?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Tuesday after President Trump ruled out using force to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I don't have to use force. I don't want to use force. I won't use force. All the United States is asking for is a place called Greenland," Trump said at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.

Trump Eyes Greenland Negotiations, Sees Ukraine-Russia Deal as Close

While the statement helped ease tensions between the U.S., Greenland, and the European Union, Trump added that he would seek immediate negotiations to begin the process of acquiring Greenland and highlighted the territory's importance to national security.

Meanwhile, Trump noted that a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia was reasonably close ahead of meetings with the presidents of both countries later this week. "If they don't get this done, they are stupid," he said.

data.title: Serbia Selects Archer Aviation (ACHR) as Preferred Air Taxi Partner Ahead of EXPO 2027
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR) has secured a new government-backed partner in Europe as Serbia moves to bring air taxis to Belgrade ahead of EXPO 2027. The country selected Archer as its preferred electric air taxi partner and signed an agreement to showcase the company's Midnight aircraft.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Serbia plans to use EXPO 2027 as a test case for new transport ideas as it prepares to host millions of visitors from more than 130 countries. As a result, air taxis are set to play a visible role during the event, which runs from May through August 2027.

Under the agreement, Serbia holds an option to purchase up to 25 Midnight aircraft within its legal framework. While the deal does not yet confirm firm orders, it adds another clear demand signal for Archer as it works toward commercial launch.

Archer Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein said, "The Republic of Serbia presents another demand signal for eVTOL aircraft, and another opportunity to deploy our aircraft and build operational expertise."
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Trump's Credit Card Rate Cap Would be an 'Economist Disaster,' Warns Jamie Dimon
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon cautioned that President Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates would lead to an "economic disaster."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"In the worst case, you'd have to have a drastic reduction of the credit card business," Dimon said during a panel at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland. "I mean drastic, I mean like 10%, I mean like 80%. You would remove credit from 80% of Americans and that is their backup credit."

Dimon Says U.S. Should Test Capping Credit Card Interest Rates

At the same time, Dimon suggested, perhaps jokingly, that the U.S. should test the cap in two states, Vermont and Massachusetts. Senators Bernie Sanders of Vermont and Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts have both backed a bill that would cap credit card interest rates at 10% for five years, although Dimon didn't explicitly mention them.

Dimon added that retailers, restaurants, travel companies, and schools would be the biggest losers of the cap, not credit card companies.

data.title: TikTok Removes a Key U.S. Hurdle and Steps Up Its E-Commerce Push
data.description:
TikTok is nearing the end of years of U.S. risk tied to its ownership and data policies. According to The Information, the company is set to complete this week the sale of its U.S. data security arm to a new joint venture. As a result, the deal removes a major concern that kept many large brands on the sidelines.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For clarification, the deal does not transfer full ownership, since ByteDance will continue to control TikTok's core business while Oracle (ORCL) and other partners hold a majority stake in the U.S. data oversight venture.

The joint venture will oversee data safety, content review, and system security for U.S. users. At the same time, ByteDance keeps control of TikTok's main revenue businesses and future profits. This structure allows TikTok to keep growing while easing pressure from U.S. lawmakers. One agency executive said the ban threat was "the biggest obstacle" for brands last year. Now, that concern has largely faded.

TikTok Shop Targets Major Brands

With the deal nearing completion, TikTok is turning its focus to online sales. Specifically, the company has launched a new effort called Project Horizon to grow TikTok Shop in the U.S. Under the plan, TikTok will work with 100 e-commerce agencies. Each agency must recruit at least 30 brands that already sell over $10 million per year on platforms like Amazon (AMZN) and Shopify (SHOP). If those brands reach $50 million in combined TikTok Shop sales by year's end, the agencies earn a share of sales.

TikTok told agencies that Project Horizon is its top priority for TikTok Shop in 2026. The goal is to add trusted brands and expand product choice on the app. For now, many brands still see TikTok as a marketing tool rather than a sales channel. However, TikTok hopes that stronger brand trust will help keep more sales inside its own platform.

We used TipRanks' comparison Tool to align the three tickers appearing in the piece. It's a great tool to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader retail industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Oklo Stock Jumps as Five-Star Analyst Calls Meta Deal a 'Meaningful Step Forward'
data.description:
Oklo's (OKLO) shares climbed 5% early Wednesday after Bank of America (BofA) (BAC) turned bullish on the stock, calling the nuclear startup's new deal with Meta (META) a "meaningful step forward."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier this month, Oklo ignited hope in its advanced nuclear technology after it disclosed that it has secured a long-term 1.2 gigawatt supply contract from Meta. The tech supermajor also tapped two other companies for nuclear energy; they are Vistra Energy (VST) and billionaire Bill Gates-backed TerraPower.

BofA Upbeat after Oklo's 'Modest' Deal with Meta

Responding to the update, BofA analyst Dimple Gosai, who was previously bearish Oklo due to investor concern about the startup's capital expenditure and the timing of its power purchase agreements with clients — upgraded Oklo from Hold to Buy.

Gosai raised her OKLO price target by 14% from $111 per share to $127, implying more than 41% upside. The analyst noted that while the capacity to be supplied is "modest," the deal marks a "meaningful step forward" and offers concrete evidence of Oklo's execution and its partner's commitment.

Furthermore, the BofA analyst noted that Meta's decision to invest capital years before the project comes online highlights how critical nuclear power has become as AI and data center energy needs ramp up.

Oklo's Losses Widen

The analyst's upbeat comment comes even as Oklo in its recent third-quarter results released in November reported a worse-than-expected net loss. Gosai previously said Oklo has made "constructive" progress by securing regulatory approvals, thereby reducing the risks associated with its fuel source.

Oklo, which remains unprofitable, broke ground on its first Aurora nuclear reactor plant in late September. The startup has said it is working to deliver its first nuclear power plant by 2028.

Is Oklo a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, analysts' consensus rating for Oklo remains a Moderate Buy. This is based on eight Buys and four Holds issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average OKLO price target of $126.89 implies about 41% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs as Trump Says He 'Won't Use Force' to Acquire Greenland in Davos Speech
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened Wednesday higher after President Trump said the U.S. won't use force to acquire Greenland during a speech at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I don't have to use force. I don't want to use force. I won't use force. All the United States is asking for is a place called Greenland," Trump said. The clarification helped ease tensions after he declined to rule out force earlier this week.

Trump Pushes Greenland Talks as EU Weighs Retaliation

Trump added that he would seek immediate negotiations to acquire the self-governing island and stressed its importance for U.S. national security.

Trump's push for Greenland has drawn sharp criticism from European leaders. The European Union is set to meet on Thursday to discuss countermeasures, which include a $108 billion tariff package on U.S. goods and the anti-coercion instrument.
